name: histolab
tagline: Reference doc for the histolab Python library: WSI tile extraction, tissue masking, and stain normalization for H&E slides.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped documentation skill with no code-execution risk. Only installs standard PyPI packages (histolab, pooch) and links to the legitimate OpenSlide project; no network exfiltration, secrets access, or hidden instructions found. Main weakness is length: the SKILL.md itself runs ~714 lines, well past the ~500-line budget, so it front-loads more co

highlights.summary: Main file is verbose (714 lines) with many full code blocks inline even though five reference files already exist for deep detail — some of that content could have been pushed to references to keep th
Re-running extraction workflows against the same processed_path/output directory is never addressed — unclear whether tiles get overwritten, duplicated, or skipped on a second run.
File is 714 lines total, well above the ~500-line budget, despite delegating deep detail to five separate reference files.
